about.biography:
Hi, I'm Ushaswi, a B.Tech CSE (AI & ML) student with a deep love for art in all its forms. From paintings and crafts to experimenting with new materials and styles, I simply enjoy creating. As an only child, art became my companion from a very young age, and over the years it has remained one of my favorite ways to spend time and express myself.

I don't limit myself to a single style or medium. I enjoy working with acrylic paints, pencil, molds, crafts, and whatever sparks my curiosity next. Nature, everyday life, faith, and random ideas often inspire me to start something new, and I love exploring wherever that inspiration leads.

I'm not trying to create something "unique" just for the sake of being different. Instead, I hope my work makes people pause for a moment, feel a sense of wonder, and experience the kind of nostalgia that reminds them of a beautiful memory or a familiar feeling. If someone looks at one of my creations and simply thinks, "This is beautiful," then I've achieved what I wanted.

I'm still a student, and art is a personal passion rather than a profession. I don't create for awards or recognition—I create because I genuinely enjoy the process. I hope to keep learning, experimenting, and making art for as long as I can.

Outside of art, I enjoy playing outdoor games, listening to music, watching Netflix, and spending time with my friends (usually by playfully annoying them).

Thanks for visiting! 😆
about.statement: Art is not what you see, but what you make others see. The canvas is a mirror, and every viewer brings their own reflection.

studioInfo.address: Available for studio visits by appointment

formFields.name: message
formFields.label: Message
formFields.placeholder: Tell me about your project, idea, or just say hello...

seo.title: Ushaswi Potlapally — Portfolio
seo.description: Artist portfolio featuring paintings, sketches, portraits, watercolors, acrylics, DIY crafts, and handmade decor. Explore original artworks and commission inquiries.

behindTheScenes.title: Process in Motion
behindTheScenes.subtitle: Real-time and time-lapse glimpses into the studio—messy, meditative, and magical moments.

items.title: Morning Sketch Routine
items.description: 30 minutes of graphite before coffee. No reference, just muscle memory and whatever's on my mind.

items.title: Watercolor Wet-on-Wet
items.description: Letting pigment flow and bloom. The paper does half the work—you just guide the water.

items.title: Macramé Knot by Knot
items.description: Hours of repetitive knotting become meditation. Square knots, spiral knots, gathering knots—rhythm in rope.

items.title: Miniature Sculpting
items.description: Tiny tools, infinite patience. A mushroom cap no bigger than a fingernail, gills carved one by one.

items.title: Pressed Flower Process
items.description: Weeks of pressing, careful arrangement, resin mixing. Preserving a moment of spring forever.

items.title: Canvas Layering Time-lapse
items.description: From stained underpainting to final highlights. Six hours compressed—color building, form emerging.
